executive_summary: The implementation for iteration 000046 (parallax CLI — ltx23 ia2v support) is complete and correct. All eight functional requirements are satisfied. The `--audio` flag has been added to `parallax create video`, guarded with the three required validations (wrong model, missing file, missing --input), passed through to the runner, included in the async args payload, and the `_ltx23` runner correctly branches to `ia2v.run()` when audio is provided. The only deviation is that the `_ltx23` signature omits the `s` (steps) positional parameter specified in FR-6 — it is silently absorbed by `**_`, which is functionally harmless because none of the ltx23 pipelines accept a `steps` argument.

verification_by_fr.note: `audio: Annotated[Optional[str], typer.Option('--audio', ...)] = None` added to `create_video` in cli/commands/create.py.
`_AUDIO_SUPPORTED_MODELS = {'ltx23'}` guard present; prints exact error and raises typer.Exit(code=1).
`_Path(audio).is_file()` check present; prints `Error: audio file not found: <path>` and exits 1.
`if input is None` check after audio validation; prints `Error: --audio requires --input (image).` and exits 1.
`video.RUNNERS[model](mdir=mdir, ..., audio=audio, ...)` — audio is passed to the runner; other runners absorb it via `**_`.

`_ltx23` accepts `audio=None` and `**_` as required. However the signature omits `s` (steps) specified in the PRD spec `(*, mdir, prompt, image, w, h, n, f, s, c, seed, audio=None, **_)` — `s` goes into `**_` instead. Functionally harmless: no ltx23 pipeline has a `steps` parameter. The ia2v branch is implemented correctly.

ia2v branch calls `ia2v.run(models_dir=mdir, prompt=prompt, image=image, audio_path=audio, width=w, height=h, length=n, fps=f, cfg=c, seed=seed)` — image object is pre-loaded by the caller, all required arguments are passed.
`args` dict in async dispatch includes `'audio': audio`.

verification_by_us.note: All CLI-level ACs implemented: parameter added (AC01 structure), backwards-compatible default None (AC02), wrong-model guard (AC03), missing-file guard (AC04), missing-input guard (AC05). Syntax compiles without errors (AC06). AC01 end-to-end execution requires GPU runtime and cannot be verified statically.

AC01–AC06 verified in code. `_ltx23` accepts `audio=None` (AC01); ia2v.run() called with correct args (AC02); i2v path unchanged when audio=None (AC03); t2v path unchanged when both None (AC04); `**_` in signature (AC05); syntax clean (AC06). AC07 (visual terminal verification) requires GPU and is out of scope for static audit.

minor_observations: The `_ltx23` signature uses `(*, mdir, prompt, image, w, h, n, f, c, seed, audio=None, **_)` — the `s` (steps) positional parameter from the PRD spec is absorbed by `**_`. Since no ltx23 pipeline (t2v, i2v, ia2v) accepts `steps`, this has zero functional impact and is consistent with pre-existing ltx23 runner behaviour.
US-002-AC07 and US-001-AC01 (visual/end-to-end verification producing a real .mp4) cannot be assessed without a GPU environment. These should be validated manually before merging.
The `_AUDIO_SUPPORTED_MODELS` set is defined at module level in create.py. If future models gain audio support, adding the model name to that set is the only change required — good extensibility.
Validation order in `create_video` is: wrong model → missing file → missing input. This is the correct order (model mismatch is cheapest to detect and most likely to be a user error).
conclusions_and_recommendations: The implementation is production-ready for merge pending GPU runtime verification of the ia2v end-to-end path (US-001-AC01, US-002-AC07). FR-6 has a minor deviation (missing `s` parameter in the explicit signature) that has no functional effect and can be left as-is or cleaned up as a trivial cosmetic change in the Refactor phase. No blocking issues were found.
